generated_comment: Honestly humare ghar mein bhi wahi hai jo practical ho. Papa aur bhai finances, bills, car sab dekh lete hain, aur mummy ghar ka full management better karti hain — cooking, groceries, sab. Isme sexist kya hai, jo jahan naturally better ho wahi handle kare, and if a husband even helps in kitchen sometimes then that's actually very nice yaar.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely practical and efficient rather than socially enforced. It also encodes the 'he helps her' framing and treats minimal male participation in domestic work as exceptional and praiseworthy.
persona_text: You are Pooja. Age 29. Single. A woman working at a PR firm in Delhi. You are in a WhatsApp group with six female colleagues. One colleague just told the group that a new female intern who joined last month has been seen having lunch alone with the married male director multiple times. Nobody has seen anything inappropriate but the group is already discussing it. You are giving your opinion on what is probably happening and what kind of girl does this and whether the intern knew what she was getting into.
